findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\].*(append|initialSegmentFamily)|toPath_append|endpoint_append|initialSegmentFamily_zero|initialSegmentFamily_one|toPath_initialSegmentFamily|endpoint_initialSegmentFamily|continuous_initialSegmentFamily" code/TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/BasedPath.lean` shows exposed bodies at lines 138 and 272 and public characteristic API at lines 142, 146, 276, 280, 284, 291, 295.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from these operation bodies; if export currently needs unfolding for type alignment, restate/add characteristic lemmas using explicit casts so downstream code uses lemmas rather than unfolding the definitions.
findings.issue: `BasedPath.append` and the `BasedPath.initialSegmentFamily` wrapper are still `@[expose]`, making their implementations part of the exported reduction surface even though users have endpoint/toPath/zero/one/continuity lemmas.

summary: The characteristic lemmas for the based-path operations are present, but several implementation bodies remain exposed despite not being a computation target for the roadmap API.
